MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Recission Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Feb-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF NICOLAS GONZALEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF RECISSION CAUSE OF ACTION.

Appearances: Mark J. Rice of the Law Offices of McNeil Silveira Rice & Wiley (415.472.3434) for defendants Zoe's Luck, LLC and Shane Dalton; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden on all issues for summary adjudication listed in the Notice of Motion. Rescission is available to a party who has no knowledge of dual representation. Vice v. Thacker (1947) 30 Cal.2d 84. Plaintiff provides no evidence that he had no knowledge of Mr. West's dual representation (See Gonzalez Declaration paragraph 8) and fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a written disclosure in English accompanied by oral explanation of the terms in Spanish amounts to "no knowledge" warranting rescission as a matter of law. Plaintiff further provides no evidence that Castillo was buyer's agent, i.e. that Castillo was a dual agent for Gonzalez and buyer. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that undisclosed alleged co-agency of West and Castillo for Gonzalez is basis for rescission. As to the 23rd affirmative defense, while it is mentioned in the Notice of Motion and briefly addressed in the Points and Authorities without any reference to the Separate Statement, it is not listed in the Separate Statement. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Jill K. Cohoe (415-400-0890) for Defendant/Moving party Vanguard Properties.
Kathryn Lloyd (925-548-9441) CSR #5955 Court reporter.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: issued raised for the first time, viable cause, ability to allege in good faith, and relationship between Plaintiff and Castillo. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of all elements of the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action, including (1) existence of a fiduciary duty created by contract, conduct, sub agency, etc.; and (2) damages proximately caused by that breach. The damages currently alleged in paragraph 39 of the operative complaint are conclusory and appear to sound in fraud as the only factual support for "coercion" found elsewhere in the Fourth Amended Complaint is based on misrepresentation and concealment, i.e. the gravamen on the complete cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, as currently alleged, sounds in fraud. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
